name: jose
usage_summary: Used in exactly one place: packages/cli-kit/src/private/node/session/exchange.ts. The sole call is `jose.decodeJwt(result.id_token).sub` on line 218, which decodes a JWT id_token to extract the `sub` (user ID) claim during an OAuth token exchange. No JWT verification (signature checking) is performed — only payload decoding.
how_to_remove: Replace the single `jose.decodeJwt` call with a two-line inline decoder: the JWT payload is the base64url-encoded middle segment of the token, so `JSON.parse(Buffer.from(token.split('.')[1], 'base64url').toString()).sub` is equivalent. Remove the `import * as jose from 'jose'` import and the `"jose"` entry from cli-kit's package.json. No other files are affected.
replacement_reason: The only usage is decoding (not verifying) a JWT payload, which requires nothing more than a base64url decode plus JSON.parse — both available in Node.js built-ins (Buffer). The full jose library (JOSE standard: JWS, JWE, JWK, JWT) is massive overkill for this single decode-only call.
